questions.question: Which of the compounds when brominated turns to meso $2, 3\text{ di bromobutane}$ ?
questions.explanation: The bromination of alkenes proceeds via anti-addition. When $Br_2$ adds to trans-2-butene, the anti-addition mechanism results in a meso compound because the resulting chiral centers have internal symmetry. Conversely, the addition of $Br_2$ to cis-2-butene would produce a racemic mixture of enantiomers.

questions.question: From following reactions, which reaction does not give "Benzene"?
questions.explanation: Options A, B, and C represent standard laboratory preparations of benzene through decarboxylation, reduction of diazonium salts, and reduction of phenol with zinc, respectively. In contrast, the reaction of phenol with chromic acid ($H_2CrO_4$) is an oxidation process that yields $p$-benzoquinone. Therefore, reaction D does not produce benzene.

questions.question: What is the major product in the following reaction? [Diagram] $\text{CH}_3 - \text{C}(\text{H}) - \text{CH} = \text{CH}_2 ightarrow \text{HX} ightarrow ?$
questions.explanation: The reaction follows Markovnikov's rule, where the electrophile ($H^+$) adds to the carbon with more hydrogen atoms ($C_1$) to form a secondary carbocation. The nucleophile ($X^-$) then attacks the $C_2$ position, resulting in 2-halo-3-methylbutane as the product. While carbocation rearrangement to a tertiary center is possible, the direct Markovnikov addition product is represented by option D.
